title: Lecture 1: Combinatorial Foundations of Probability
text: Probability Theory by  Carlos Buitrago  · Fall 2026

title: Lecture 1: Combinatorial Foundations of Probability
text:
1.1 Example
A password consists of 4 digits.
How many passwords exist?
We have
\[
10^4=10000.
\]
The probability of guessing correctly in one attempt is
\[
\frac{1}{10000}.
\]
